EPISODE_NARRATIVE: AN OUTBREAK OF WATERSPOUTS, A TOTAL OF 12 TO 15, WAS OBSERVED BY THE U.S. COAST GUARD OFFICIALS ALONG THE NORTH COAST OF PUERTO RICO.

NUMEROUS SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS AFFECTED THE NORTH SECTION OF PUERTO RICO. THE HEAVY RAINFALL CAUSED PROBLEMS ALONG THE NORTH COAST OF PUERTO RICO FROM CAROLINA TO THE WEST TO ARECIBO. HOWEVER, THE MOST AFFECTED AREA WAS THE METROPOLITAN AREA OF SAN JUAN. AMONG THE SECTORS AFFECTED BY FLASH FLOOD IN SAN JUAN WERE: CAIMITO, CUPEY, PUERTO NUEVO, RIO PIEDRAS, TRUJILLO ALTO, OCEAN PARK AND VILLA PALMERAS.

A STATIONARY COLD FRONT ALONG THE NORTH COAST OF PUERTO RICO AND THE U.S. VIRGIN ISLANDS SERVED AS A MECHANISM FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF NUMEROUS SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS. HEAVY RAIN AFFECTED MAINLY THE EASTERN-INTERIOR AND NORTHEAST SECTIONS OF PUERTO RICO. THERE WERE FLOODED ROADS AND STREETS IN THE FOLLOWING MUNICIPALITIES: CAGUAS, CAYEY, CIDRA, NAGUABO, FAJARDO AND CEIBA.
Civil defense reported that Road 430, at Barrio Ovejas was partly impassable due to river Rio Grande de Anasco overtopping the bridge.
An outbreak of Saharan Dust combined with strong high pressure produced very hazy skies and restricted visibilities across the U.S. Virgin Islands.
Civil Defense reported minor flooding in several neighborhoods in Caguas.
Civil Defense reported some power lines that were struck by lightning in Comerio.

Heavy rain affected the eastern interior and northeast section. Three houses were reported flooded in Luquillo. River Mameyes level rose and a nearby creek was reported out of its banks over Highway 3 near the Luquillo Public Beach. Landslides were reported at the Caribbean National Forest, Road 191; and at Las Piedras, Road 31. Barrio Mameyes, La Vega sector was reported incommunicated due to river Mameyes flooding the road. Heavy rain was also reported in Ceiba, Naguabo, and Rio Grande.

At Las Cuevas sector in Loiza a small pig farm and house suffered some damage due to strong gusty winds. The zinc roof of an old wood structure which served as a pork building was ripped off. Also a few zinc roof liners of two story concrete structure were blown away.
Civil Defense reported street flooding and that river Rio Culebrinas was out of its banks.
Local police reported street flooding in Hormigueros.
Civil defense reported small stream and road flooding affecting Highway 2 in Mayaguez and a mudslide on road 348.
A PILOT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT TWO MILES NORTHEAST OF LUQUILLO IN NORTHEAST PUERTO RICO.

A COLD FRONT MOVING ACROSS PUERTO RICO AND THE U.S. VIRGIN ISLANDS BROUGHT ABOUT THE DEVELOPMENT OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS ACROSS THE INTERIOR AND NORTH SECTIONS OF PUERTO RICO. MANY FLOODING PROBLEMS WERE REPORTED IN THE SAN JUAN METROPOLITAN AREA, SUCH AS STREETS AND ROADS FLOODING AND FLOODED HOUSES.
THE CONTINUATION OF THE PREVIOUS DAY HEAVY RAINS KEPT CAUSING FLOODING PROBLEMS ACROSS THE SAN JUAN METROPOLITAN AREA.
Civil defense reported street flooding in Guanica.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in Humacao.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in Luquillo.
Civil Defense reported a mudslide on Road 181 in Gurabo.

Civil Defense from San Juan municipality reported flooding at Puerto Nuevo, Reparto Metropolitano, Guaynabo, and in front of the Oso Blanco State Penitentiary. Cars were reported underwater at the Martinez Nadal Avenue due to a small stream that went out of its banks.
Civil Defense reported a downed tree in Humacao.
Strong...gusty winds downed power lines and trees in Lares. Road 431 was reported impassable.
A pilot reported a waterspout 15-18 miles west of Vieques moving west.
Two waterspouts were reported north of Culebra moving northwest.

Civil Defense reported thunderstorm, mudslides, and high river levels in Lares. Frequent lightning, downed trees, more than one inch of rain. Road 470 was reported impassable due to downed trees. River Culebrinas level was reported rising at San Sebastian. Lares Civil Defense reported that River Guajataca was out of its banks. Several small streams were also out of their banks like Quebrada Anon, De Los Muertos, Del Puente del Caballito, and Monte Bello. Four inches of rainfall were reported during the last hour. Mudslides on road 436. On road 128, km. 40.6 a house slid down hill.
A pilot reported several waterspouts three miles east southeast of Culebra.
Several waterspouts were reported 5 miles northeast of Culebra. Movement was west northwest.
The Carraizo Water Reservoir gate 6 was opened at 6 meters.

Civil Defense reported flooding in the Gurabo and Caguas area. At 2300 AST some houses in Caguas were reported flooded with 3-4 feet of water. Landslides were reported in Caguas, Guayama, and Canovanas. The rains continued overnight in the Caguas Gurabo area.
At 1400 AST the Roosevelt Roads Naval Base observer reported a funnel cloud south of the station moving to the southwest.
The cooperative observer at Cerro Maravilla in Villalba reported quarter size hail.
A pilot reported a waterspout two miles east of the Fajardo lighthouse.
Police reported that coastal flooding continues along road 187 in Pinones. The eastern end of Pinones were reported completely under water.
Police reported coastal flooding along road 686 near Manati.
Civil Defense reported strong gusty winds around 11 AST which damaged a hardware store and downed several power lines.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains at Adjuntas and along the slopes between Adjuntas, Ponce, Penuelas, Juana Diaz, Guayanilla, Yauco, Guanica, and San German.
The Civil Defense from the Ponce municipality that two persons were washed when River Portuguez crested during the afternoon. Both persons were rescued.
Civil Defense reported some broken tree branches in Utuado.
Civil Defense reported moderate to heavy rains in Mayaguez, Maricao, Las Marias, and Utuado. In Anasco road 430 was reported flooded, and roads 405 and 109 with mudslides.

HEAVY RAINS OVER THE NORTH COAST OF PUERTO RICO. THE RAINS AFFECTED MAINLY THE MUNICIPALITIES OF DORADO AND VEGA ALTA. CIVIL DEFENSE OFFICIALS REPORTED THE EVACUATION OF 16 HOMES IN BARRIO BRENAS IN VEGA ALTA.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A PILOT ONE AND A HALF MILES WEST OF CULEBRA, PUERTO RICO.

HEAVY RAINS AFFECTED THE SOUTH AND WEST SECTIONS OF PUERTO RICO CAUSING A RIVER (RIO GUAYO) TO OVERFLOW IN JUANA DIAZ. THESE RAINS ALSO CAUSED THE BLOCKAGED OF ROADS #402 AND #109 IN ANASCO DUE TO PONDING OF WATER AND OVERLOWING OF SMALL STREAMS.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED SEVERAL MUDSLIDES ON ROAD #109 IN MOCA AND ROAD # 119 IN SAN SEBASTIAN.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED NORTHEAST OF ROOSEVELT ROADS NAVAL BASE IN NORTHEAST PUERTO RICO.
A WATERSPOUT WAS SIGHTED 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF CULEBRA.
The FAA Flight Service Office reported a funnel cloud two miles east of the Luis Munoz Marin airport departure runway.
A pilot reported a waterspout 6 miles northwest of San Juan.
The San Sebastian Civil Defense reported hail, heavy rains, and high winds.
Lares Civil Defense reported very heavy rain and strong thunderstorms.
Civil Defense reported hail at Las Marias municipality, Barrio Anones.

Civil Defense reported that in Arecibo, one person was confirmed dead, and one missing. Two men in a 27 footer vessel went out to sea. 15 foot swells came up suddenly and broke the boat in two pieces. One drowned body recovered, the other was missing, presumably drowned.
Carolina Civil defense reported that the sea intruded into the Pinones area again.
Civil Defense reported that two homes near the river Mameyes in Rio Grande were flooded with 3 feet of water when the river went out of its banks.
Civil defense reported that in Humacao, at Las Piedras sector the road 31 was impassable.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains over Ceiba.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Barranquitas and Cidra municipalities.
Civil Defense reported rivers near bankfull in Canovanas, and some rivers out of their banks in Loiza.
Civil Defense reported that road 906 was impassable at km. 1.1 in Humacao.
Civil Defense reported minor flooding of low lying areas in Toa Baja.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in Jayuya.
Mayaguez Civil Defense reported very heavy rains.
A pilot reported a waterspout 3 miles south of Culebra.
The Roosevelt Roads Naval Base observer reported a funnel cloud.
A pilot reported two waterspouts 13 miles east of San Juan moving to the northwest.
Civil Defense reported street flooding in Condado.
Civil Defense reported a waterspout in the Catano vicinity, and over San Juan Bay.
Civil Defense reported very heavy rain in Vega Baja and some minor flooding of low lying areas.
Civil Defense reported small hail at Barrio Hatillo in Villalba.
Civil Defense reported several downed trees in the municipality Coamo. Power lines were downed and one automobile was damaged by a fallen tree.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains across eastern interior Puerto Rico. The municipalities of Humacao, Las Piedras, and Rio Grande have reported flooding of some low lying urban areas.
A mariner reported a waterspout just east of Fajardo.
Civil Defense reported flooding in Humacao.

Ponce police informed at 2150 AST that road 1 near the airport was flooded...stalled vehicles were also reported. Civil Defense indicated several rivers were out of their banks in the municipalities of Ponce, Penuelas, Villalba, Juana Diaz, Coamo and Santa Isabel.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in Toa Alta.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in Vega Baja.

A FEMA meteorologist in Miramar called the NWSFO with a report of a funnel cloud over San Juan at 1420 AST. The funnel cloud was visible from the NWSFO location at Carolina. Another NWSFO observer on his way to the NWSFO from Bayamon to Carolina reported two funnel clouds, one extending down to surface, the other aloft.  A municipal Civil Defense volunteer from Catano reported that from the Juana Matos I Housing Project where he was located he was able to see only one funnel cloud looking north towards the San Juan Bay. The funnel or waterspout was moving towards the Catano shore line or south, and afterwards it was only wind and rain. He thought the waterspout touched down in the middle of the San Juan Bay in front of the fish market.

Local T.V. Channels 2 and 4 presented on their 17 AST evening news  wind damage videos at the Old Navy Base in San Juan, Miramar sector. Snapped branches, downed trees, and some partially damaged cars were the result of thunderstorm winds earlier in the afternoon.
A waterspout was reported near Ceiba's east coast.
Civil Defense reported downed power lines in Carolina.
Civil Defense reported street flooding in the Puerto Nuevo sector of San Juan.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ONE MILE EAST OF CULEBRA, PUERTO RICO.
AN OBSERVER AT BORINQUEN UNICOM PORT AUTHORITY IN AGUADILLA REPOTED A WATERSPOUT APROXIMATELY 10 MILES NORTHWEST OF BORINQUEN AIR FIELD. THE MOVEMENT WAS NORTH-NORTHWEST AT AN UNDETERMINED SPEED.

A COLD FRONT ACROSS THE MONA PASSAGE INDUCED THE DEVELOPMENT OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS OVER THE NORTH, WEST AND INTERIOR SECTIONS OF PUERTO RICO. THE HARDEST HIT AREA WAS THE NORTHWEST OFPUERTO RICO WHERE CIVIL DEFENSE OFFICIALS REPORTED 20 HOMES INUNDATED IN HATILLO AND HIGHWAY #2 WAS INTRASITABLE.

Tropical wave moving across Puerto Rico produced heavy rainfall across many areas. Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Fajardo, Loiza, Luquillo, Rio Grande and Vieques. Moderate rains were reported in Trujillo Alto, Canovanas, and Carolina.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in the Humacao and Yabucoa area. Also San Lorenzo, Juncos and Las Piedras were receiving heavy rains.
Civil Defense reported tidal over-wash at Loiza. Large northerly swells generated by a low pressure area north of Puerto Rico were arriving to our coastal waters.
Heavy rains were reported in Caguas. Some low lying areas were flooded along the autopista.

Civil Defense reported that river Rio Coamo in Santa Isabel was overflowing its banks. Highway 545 was flooded and closed off the Paso Seco sector. Flooding was mostly due to runoff according to Civil Defense. Highway 536 was reported with flooding problems and river Rio Descalabrado was reported rising.
The Puerto Nuevo river was reported out of its banks.
Civil defense reported coastal flooding in the Pinones area. Road 187 was closed off to traffic.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in Lares.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in Las Marias.
Civil Defense reported tidal over-wash at Arecibo. Large northerly swells generated by low pressure system north of Puerto Rico were arriving to our coastal waters.
Observers from the Roosevelt Roads Naval Base reported two waterspouts just south of Naguabo area.
A large waterspout was reported by the Luis Munoz Marin Air Traffic Control Tower personnel seven miles northeast of the airport.
A waterspout was reported one and a half mile southeast of Culebra.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain and thunder in Orocovis, Aibonito,Barranquitas, Ciales, and Morovis.

Heavy rains were reported in the municipality of Gurabo during the afternoon. At 1310 AST Civil Defense reported 2.20 inches of rain during the past hour, and 1.10 during the past half hour. Road 941 was reported with minor flooding problems at 1420 AST. At 1600 AST Civil Defense reported thre flooded houses at Barrio Toqui, Robles, and Hato Nuevo. At 1730 AST Civil Defense reported 5.20 inches of rain during the past two hours. The rain was so intense between 12 noon and 1400 AST that water six feet deep was in the streets. The municipality of caguas reported 4.50 inches of rain in two and half hours.
Civil Defense reported a flooded house in Hatillo.

Coastal Flood Watch issued for north and northeast coasts of Puerto Rico, Vieques and Culebra. Large northerly swells generated from Hurricane Lili reached the area. A resident of the Pinones sector in Carolina reported swells washing over beach and road 187 around 19 AST on 10/23/96. Civil defense reported minor coastal flooding in Loiza, Pinones, Fajardo and Anasco.
Coastal flooding was reported at Pinones, road 187 was covered with water shortly after high tide and was closed much of the afternoon.
Civil Defense reported flooding in Bayamon.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in San Juan, Guaynabo, Bayamon, Toa Alta, Toa Baja, and Dorado.

Flash flooding was reported at Adjuntas municipality during the afternoon when heavy rains caused river Rio Grande de Arecibo in Adjuntas to overtop the bridge Puente de la Playita. A person tried to cross the bridge in his car when a water crest came and washed him and the car downriver.
Civil defense reported heavy rains in Arecibo.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Camuy.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Toa Baja.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Maricao and Las Marias.

Several roads and streets along interior Puerto Rico were reported impassable due to flooding of small streams. Also, La Plata Dam gates were opened earlier and the river level rose steadily for several hours, posing a flood threat to the municipalities of Toa Alta, Toa Baja, and Dorado.
A waterspout was reported in the vicinity of Culebra.

Civil Defense reported street flooding in some sectors of Carolina and Canovanas. Several houses were reported flooded at Barrio San Isidro in Canovanas. A small stream in the Los Angeles urbanization was reported out of its banks.
